event: slipping on wet grass and falling on his left side
undisplaced fracture of his left acetabulum
both the anterior and posterior columns
an 18 g intravenous cannula was inserted
warmed Hartmanns one litre infusion was commenced
referred to the Orthopaedic on call team
his blood pressure dropped to 75/51 mm Hg
treated for sepsis in the Emergency department
arterial blood pressure was 90/60 mm Hg
left lower abdominal quadrant and upper thigh were now swollen with ecchymosis
diagnosis of haemorrhagic shock was made
two adult therapeutic doses of platelets
not tightened as this was a lateral compression injury
on call radiology team and vascular surgeons were contacted
CT abdomen confirmed the fracture of the left acetabulum
significant haematoma on left pelvic sidewall
8 mm enhancing nodule suggestive of an internal iliac artery pseudoaneurysm
ill-defined blush of contrast within the haematoma
extra-peritoneal haematoma inseparable from the bladder
transferred to the intensive care unit
CT Angiogram demonstrated no arterial bleed
gelfoam embolization of anterior and posterior division segmental branches of the left internal iliac artery
developed uncontrollable bleeding from his pin site
needed removal and continuous pressure applied to the wound
as a result of his chronic liver disease
